name: phrygian-slab
description: Concrete sub bass for Rust Protocol. Designed from knowledge/sound-design/synthesis-notes.md (dark cutoff, sub as physical weight) + knowledge/band-lore/manifesto.md (bass as physical experience) + knowledge/music-theory/chord-vocabulary.md (anchoring the A Phrygian i chord). No detune — unified pitch for maximum fundamental weight. OSC1 saw + OSC2 pulse for harmonic mass without beating. Sub maxed. Filter barely open. Subtle overdrive instead of filter resonance. Mod wheel opens the filter live when the bFlat-II needs to cut through.
design_sources: knowledge/sound-design/synthesis-notes.md — Bass: keep cutoff dark, sub should feel more than be heard
knowledge/band-lore/manifesto.md — bass frequencies as physical experience, not just sound
knowledge/music-theory/chord-vocabulary.md — flat-II (Bb) move is the A Phrygian signature; bass anchors i (A)
knowledge/music-theory/scales-and-modes.md — Phrygian heavy cadence: Em–F–Em or Am–Bb–Am

design_rationale.why_no_detune: Detuned oscillators create beating that diffuses the fundamental. At 95 BPM the bass notes need to land hard and stay solid.
design_rationale.why_pulse_not_second_saw: SAW+PULSE gives harmonic variety (odd harmonics dominant in pulse vs all harmonics in saw) without the pitch-beating of two detuned saws.
design_rationale.why_no_filter_snap: The previous preset used a filter snap (high env1 amount) which thinned the sub on attack. This preset keeps the filter static and dark — the weight comes from the sub oscillator, not a filter sweep.
design_rationale.why_overdrive: NRPN 35 (Overdrive) adds even-harmonic saturation that gives the bass presence in the mid register without opening the filter and losing sub weight.
design_rationale.why_no_reverb: Reverb smears low frequencies and adds mud. The physical bass experience requires dry signal.

nrpn_dump.label: Osc2 Fine Tune 0 cents — no detune, unified pitch
Osc2 Shape full pulse — odd harmonics, different texture from OSC1
Sub Osc Level max — this IS the physical weight
Filter Cutoff 24pct — dark, passes fundamental+few harmonics only
Filter Resonance minimal — no resonance hump competing with sub
Filter Key Amount moderate — higher notes slightly brighter
Vintage 40 — slight analog instability, not perfectly static
Overdrive subtle saturation — presence without opening filter
Env1 Decay 55pct — short filter punch on attack
Env1 Sustain 0 — filter falls back to dark baseline
Env1 Amount bipolar +10 above center 128 — tiny filter nudge on attack
Env2 Attack very fast — tiny ramp to kill the hard click
Env2 Sustain 78pct — notes hold their weight
Env Routing Env1 to Filter Env2 to Amp
Glide Off — 95 BPM bass needs tight note separation
Reverb Off — reverb smears sub frequencies
Mod1 Dest Cutoff — wheel opens filter for the bFlat-II move
Mod1 Amount bipolar +52 above center — significant sweep range
